rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions.
Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for such assignments.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in declarations and expressions.
Validate that guarantee expressions have consistent types on both sides of operators.
Prevent invalid type assignments by matching declared helper types with expression results.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for assignments.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current scope.
Do not mix invalid or mismatched types in AGREE expressions without proper declaration alignment.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Component type vs implementation scope distinctions beyond visibility
